comment.body: I am also learning. My 2 cents - I believe main point is to compare far out of money puts to near the ATM puts. If 5dp are increasing compared to 20dp then market thinks a big move is more likely to happen. Other the other hand if far out of money puts are getting sold more then market thinks big move will most likely not happen.

comment.body:
When I trade weekly contracts that expire Friday. My rule is don’t pay more than $1. 

So whenever I see puts $10 OTM the same cost as calls $7 OTM. I always go cash or calls. I see this and then I see the tariff headlines… it feels like bait

comment.body:
Purely from risk reward I’m talking. Market has an upward bias over time, right? If on a given week like this week. I can get a call $7 OTM for the same cost as a put $10 OTM in a market that isn’t trending down and well supported. If I were going to take a position, I’d take the call

But if I believe the market was going to go down. Which it could. I’d just stay cash because puts are too expensive. I don’t want to take that potential loss. Especially with what Alma wrote
